subject: digest-bound queue proposal freshness

observed: CM-0021 bound a queue inventory proposal to the exact canonical and additive-update bytes available during its run.
Repository commits after CM-0021 added new queue-update records and implementation evidence, including Frontier Research terminal durability and reconciliation slices.
A previously valid proposal digest does not include files committed after that proposal was generated.
inferred: Digest validity proves integrity of the inventoried input set, not freshness or completeness relative to the current repository head.
Reviewer authorization against a stale inventory can be authentic yet insufficient for current canonical reconciliation.
proposed: Every canonical queue-change proposal must carry an inventory_head_commit and must fail closed when repository head or the queue-update tree differs before review or application.
Freshness verification should compare both the proposal's recorded head commit and a digest of the operations/queue-updates tree before mutation.
superseded: Treating an unchanged proposal digest as proof that its inventory still covers the current queue-update history.
unresolved: No canonical queue mutation is authorized by this record.
The complete current queue-update tree digest has not been generated in this connector-only run.
Authorized reviewer identity and post-application verification remain absent.

falsification_routes: Show that CM-0021 was regenerated after all listed later commits while preserving the same proposal identity and complete input manifest.
Generate the current queue-update inventory and demonstrate byte-for-byte equality with CM-0021's recorded manifest.
Prove that no later commit added or changed any queue-update input relevant to canonical projection.
privacy_boundary: No private chat text, health or pet-health material, credentials, contact data, or unreviewed uploads were incorporated.
